title: Resinous Crunchy Ayurvedic Burrata Polish
description: A monsoon-inspired Polish dish featuring creamy burrata cheese, crunchy sesame seeds, and tangy kombu-cold-extract. This ayurvedic-tamasic-friendly recipe is perfect for a light dinner or snack.
instructions: Soak the kombu sheets in water for at least 4 hours. Discard the water and extract the kombu-cold-extract.
Heat a non-stick pan over medium heat. Add the olive oil and saut9 the grilled portobello mushrooms until caramelized.
Add the chopped fresh parsley and saut9 until wilted. Set aside.
In a large bowl, combine the burrata cheese, sesame seeds, apple cider vinegar, salt, and black pepper. Mix well.
Add the saut9ed mushroom mixture and mix until combined.
Transfer the mixture to a serving dish and top with the chopped dill pickles and crushed red pepper flakes.
Garnish with additional parsley if desired. Serve immediately.
Serve with a side of crusty bread or crackers for a satisfying snack.
Store any leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.
